components.name: Primary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-weight: 600; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 18px; font-size: 0.94rem; color: #fff; background: #2a94d6; box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(42,148,214,0.30); text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }

components.name: Card
components.html:
Card Title

Supporting description for the card content.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(42,148,214,0.12); border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-card-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.97rem; margin-bottom: 8px; color: #1f2937; } .ds-card-text { font-size: 0.93rem; color: #6b7280; line-height: 1.6; }

narrative.overview: The Citum design system reflects the precision and transparency required for academic work. It avoids the neon flash of modern tech in favor of a warm, paper-like palette and crisp, legible typography.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutral Rule
rules.body: Never use pure white (#fff) for large background areas. Every surface should be tinted toward the primary hue or the warm base to reduce eye strain.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: Use subtle borders as the primary method of separation. Shadows should only be added to elements that need to appear interactive or floating.

dos: Do use the compatibility report's 'evidence-first' layout for any feature claims.
Do maintain the 64ch line length for readability.
donts: Don't use pure black (#000) for text.
Don't use 'bento box' grids with varied card sizes for core information.
